prompt: Draft a detailed surface plasmon resonance (SPR) protocol for measuring the binding kinetics (kon, koff, KD) of a therapeutic anti-IL-6 receptor (IL-6R) monoclonal antibody (e.g. tocilizumab biosimilar) using a Biacore T200. The protocol should include: (1) instrument setup: CM5 chip amine-coupling of recombinant human IL-6R-Fc, target immobilisation level 100–200 RU, (2) analyte preparation: serial dilution of antibody in HBS-EP+ buffer (0.1–100 nM), (3) single-cycle kinetics run at 25°C with 120 s association and 600 s dissociation, (4) regeneration: 10 mM glycine pH 1.5 for 30 s, (5) data fitting: 1:1 Langmuir model in Biacore Insight Evaluation software, (6) acceptance criteria: chi2 < 10 RU2, Rmax within 20% of theoretical. Include troubleshooting for common issues: mass transport limitation, non-specific binding, drift.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and SPR methodology literature; target: IL-6R; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: therapeutic antibody–cytokine receptor SPR binding assay
